_meta.purpose: Studio point-of-contact database for the monthly film-slate newsletter. The routine does NOT research contacts at run time; it looks them up here. For each studio block in the email, resolve the studio label -> company via label_to_company, then pick that company's best contact.
_meta.owner_note: Built from open-web research (trade press + LinkedIn). Most individual emails are INFERRED from each company's known corporate email pattern and are unverified. Zack's CRM contacts, when added, should be entered with status:'confirmed' and a real verified email; confirmed entries always win over speculative ones.
selection_order: 1. A contact with status='confirmed' (from the CRM) always wins.
3. Otherwise the first contact with role_type='partnerships'.
4. Publicity contacts (role_type='publicity') are a fallback only when no partnerships contact exists.
Render the chosen contact's name, title, and email. If status='speculative', tag the email/contact as unverified in the email. If email is null, render 'email: research via LinkedIn / CRM'.

field_legend.role_type: partnerships = brand/promotional/strategic partnerships or marketing lead (who you pitch a paid creator integration to). publicity = press/media-relations lead (backup only).
field_legend.status: confirmed = verified, from Good Story CRM. speculative = research-derived, may be inaccurate.
field_legend.email_type: direct = published/verified individual address. inferred = constructed from the company email pattern, NOT verified. department = shared/team inbox. none = no email available.
field_legend.confidence: high | medium | low — confidence that the PERSON currently holds this role. Email accuracy is separate (see email_type).
field_legend.primary: true on the single best default contact for the company.

_resources.name: MarketingPartnerships.com studio directory
_resources.note: Industry directory of studio promotional-partnership departments and contacts (Disney, WB, Universal, Paramount, Lionsgate, etc.). Good place to verify/update department contacts.

_resources.name: Studio careers pages
_resources.note: Job postings (sonypicturesjobs.com, careers.paramount.com, jobs.lionsgate.com, amazon.jobs) reliably name the current partnerships departments and reporting lines even when individual names aren't public.

contacts.name: Lylle Breier
contacts.title: EVP, Marketing Partnerships & Special Events, The Walt Disney Company
contacts.notes: Runs ALL marketing partnerships, brand campaigns, and product placement for feature films + streaming across Disney Live Action, Walt Disney Animation, Pixar, Marvel, Lucasfilm, 20th Century, and Disney+. Single best entry point for the whole Disney family of labels.

warner_bros.notes: Post-2025 Warner Bros. Discovery reorg: some corporate roles may use @wbd.com. Verify before sending.

contacts.name: Sarah Gregory
contacts.title: VP, Global Brand Partnerships, Warner Bros.
contacts.notes: Dedicated global brand-partnerships lead. May appear under Warner Bros. Discovery (@wbd.com).

contacts.name: Cameron Curtis
contacts.title: EVP, Worldwide Digital Marketing, Warner Bros. Pictures
contacts.notes: Restructured WB digital marketing into teams for social creative, TALENT & INFLUENCER strategy, and digital partnerships — the most relevant exec specifically for creator/YouTube integrations.

contacts.name: Josh Goldstine
contacts.title: President, Worldwide Marketing, Warner Bros. Pictures Group
contacts.notes: Top of the WB film marketing org; escalation point.

contacts.name: Jan Craft
contacts.title: VP, Global Publicity, Warner Bros. Pictures
contacts.notes: PR/publicity backup contact.

universal.notes: Corporate/UP&L staff use first.last@nbcuni.com (~96%). Some Universal Pictures film staff use lastf@universalpictures.com. Illumination & DreamWorks promotions run through Universal.

contacts.name: Stephanie Sperber
contacts.title: President, Universal Partnerships & Licensing (UP&L), NBCUniversal
contacts.notes: UP&L is Universal's single point of contact for film promotions and corporate alliances/brand partnerships across theatrical, home ent, and parks. Best entry point for Universal/DreamWorks/Illumination promo deals.

focus_features.notes: Universal's specialty label, runs its own marketing/publicity. Big promo tie-ins can also route through Universal UP&L.

contacts.name: Jason Cassidy
contacts.title: President of Marketing, Focus Features
contacts.notes: Head of Focus marketing; reports to chairman Peter Kujawski.

contacts.name: Joshua Kornblit
contacts.title: EVP, Marketing, Focus Features
contacts.notes: Day-to-day marketing/digital lead. ZoomInfo confirms j***@focusfeatures.com pattern.

contacts.name: Stephanie Phillips
contacts.title: EVP, Publicity, Focus Features
contacts.notes: PR/publicity backup.

paramount.notes: Now Paramount Skydance Corporation (2025 merger); Skydance theatrical titles flow through this org.

contacts.name: Irene Trachtenberg
contacts.title: SVP, Worldwide Marketing & Partnerships, Paramount Pictures
contacts.notes: Oversees global, multi-territory and domestic brand partnerships / promotional relationships for theatrical releases; has brokered major brand marketing support. ZoomInfo confirms i***@paramount.com pattern. Dept = Worldwide Theatrical Brand Partnerships & Promotions.

sony.notes: Primary SPE pattern is first_last@spe.sony.com (~69%). Columbia/TriStar/Screen Gems all sit under SPE marketing.

contacts.name: Jeffrey Godsick
contacts.title: EVP, Global Partnerships & Brand Strategy (and Head of Location-Based Entertainment), Sony Pictures
contacts.notes: Leads Sony's worldwide partnerships & brand strategy; active brand-integration speaker in 2026. Best senior entry point.

contacts.name: (Open / verify) EVP, Global Brand Partnerships & Promotions
contacts.title: EVP, Global Brand Partnerships & Promotions, Sony Pictures Motion Picture Group (reports to Head of Global Theatrical Marketing)
contacts.notes: Role was being actively recruited (job posting, 2026) — the dedicated film promotional-partnerships seat in Culver City (15-person team). Confirm the current name before outreach.

netflix.notes: Most common pattern is first-initial + last (e.g. mherran@netflix.com), ~65%. firstlast@netflix.com also occurs.

contacts.name: Magno Herran
contacts.title: VP, Global Brand & Partnership Marketing, Netflix
contacts.notes: Leads Netflix's global brand-marketing and partnerships team (e.g. Bridgerton x Dove, Wednesday x Wendy's). Top entry point for title-tied brand deals.

contacts.name: Chris Ficcara
contacts.title: Senior Director, Brand Partnerships, Netflix
contacts.notes: Operational brand-partnerships contact under Herran.

apple.notes: Apple is deliberately opaque about marketing contacts and rarely runs traditional promotional/brand partnerships. Apple Studios is run by Zack Van Amburg & Jamie Erlicht; the consumer-marketing chief seat has been in flux (Ricky Strauss departed May 2024). Best practical route is via the film's publicity team or the relevant talent agency rather than a studio partnerships desk.

contacts.name: (Vacant / in flux) Head of Marketing, Apple TV+
contacts.title: Head of Marketing, Apple TV+ (consumer marketing, advertising, promotions for originals)
contacts.notes: Confirm the current marketing head before any outreach. Treat Apple as a CRM/relationship-only contact until Zack provides a name.

amazon_mgm.notes: Amazon email addresses are unpredictable (corporate login @amazon.com), so individual emails should be confirmed via LinkedIn/CRM rather than inferred.

contacts.name: Lauren Anderson
contacts.title: Head of Brand & Content Innovation, Amazon MGM Studios
contacts.notes: Manages the studio's creative collaboration with brands across the Prime Video slate — sponsorships, integrations, custom content. Strongest fit for paid creator integrations.

contacts.name: Ned Mitchell
contacts.title: Head of Brand Partnerships, Originals (LatAm/Canada/Australia), Amazon MGM Studios International (LA-based)
contacts.notes: Dedicated brand-partnerships exec (LA). EU counterpart: Ludovic Attal (London).

contacts.name: Adriene Bowles
contacts.title: Head of Theatrical Publicity, Amazon MGM Studios
contacts.notes: PR/publicity backup. Top marketing = Sue Kroll (Head of Global Marketing, Film & Series).

contacts.name: Marisa Liston
contacts.title: President, Worldwide Marketing, Lionsgate Motion Picture Group
contacts.notes: Top of Lionsgate film marketing; brand-partnerships team (incl. a Director, Brand Partnerships) reports into her org.

contacts.name: Brad Haugen
contacts.title: EVP, Digital Strategy & Growth, Lionsgate
contacts.notes: Leads original digital content, brand partnerships, and creator/storytelling initiatives — relevant for YouTube/creator deals.

contacts.name: Graham Retzik
contacts.title: Head of Creative Marketing & Brand, A24
contacts.notes: Leads A24 brand + creative marketing (ZoomInfo confirms g***@a24films.com; could be graham.retzik@a24films.com). A24 does limited traditional brand-partnership deals.

skydance.notes: Merged into Paramount Skydance Corporation (2025) — Skydance theatrical films now flow through Paramount's marketing/partnerships (see 'paramount'). Skydance maintains its own franchise/corporate marketing.

contacts.name: Rebecca Mall
contacts.title: President, Cross-Company Initiatives, Franchise & Corporate Marketing, Paramount Skydance (former Skydance CMO)
contacts.notes: For a Skydance-produced theatrical title, also try the Paramount partnerships contact (Irene Trachtenberg).

legendary.notes: Production company; theatrical distribution now via Paramount (3-year deal, 2025+). Legendary runs its own marketing & partnerships.

contacts.name: Blair Rich
contacts.title: Chief Marketing & Commercial Officer, Legendary Entertainment
contacts.notes: Joined Nov 2024 from Warner Bros. (former President of Worldwide Marketing). Drives Legendary's global marketing, branding, and partnerships.

contacts.name: Brian Worsley
contacts.title: Marketing Executive (global marketing, partnerships & JVs), Legendary Entertainment
contacts.notes: Global marketing across film/TV/gaming/licensing incl. partnerships & joint ventures (joined March 2025).

blumhouse.notes: Horror label; theatrical distribution mostly via Universal. Runs its own marketing/partnerships and has a branded-content partnership with NBCUniversal.

contacts.name: Amanda Maes
contacts.title: EVP, Marketing, Blumhouse
contacts.notes: Joined Aug 2025; oversees consumer products, live experiences, PARTNERSHIPS, and brand initiatives. Reports to CMO Karen Barragan. Prior: Lionsgate & Netflix.

contacts.name: Karen Barragan
contacts.title: Chief Marketing Officer, Blumhouse
contacts.notes: Heads marketing + communications for theatrical and TV; escalation point / PR backup.
